title: Comptroller liquidation incentive set to 1.0x (zero profit) disables liquidations and risks protocol insolvency
claim: The comptroller (0x959Fb43EF08F415da0AeA39BEEf92D96f41E41b3) referenced by this kTAF cToken has liquidationIncentiveMantissa set to the minimum allowed value of 1e18 (1.0x multiplier), meaning liquidators receive exactly the same USD value of collateral as the debt they repay, providing zero economic incentive to perform liquidations.
impact: Underwater borrowing positions will not be liquidated because rational actors have no profit motive. This leads to accumulation of bad debt in all markets including kTAF. If collateral prices drop, the protocol becomes undercollateralized and depositors cannot withdraw their funds. This is a systemic insolvency risk.

steps: cast call 0x959Fb43EF08F415da0AeA39BEEf92D96f41E41b3 'liquidationIncentiveMantissa()(uint256)' --rpc-url <RPC>
Returns 1000000000000000000 (1e18 = 1.0x, zero incentive)

paths.description: Exploit path: collateral price drops below debt value, no liquidator steps in, bad debt accumulates
steps: 1. User deposits TAF as collateral and borrows another asset
2. TAF price drops below the borrow threshold
3. The position becomes eligible for liquidation
4. But with liquidationIncentive = 1.0x, liquidators have no profit motive
5. The position remains unliquidated
6. Protocol holds bad debt; depositors cannot withdraw

title: Underlying TAF token has unlimited owner mint capability creating dilution risk for kTAF depositors
claim: The underlying TAF token (AssetToken at 0xf573E6740045b5387F6d36a26B102C2adF639af5) has a public mintAssetTokens(uint256) function callable by the owner at 0x5a995f45CcE03670D94a0c89572dc74598d57581, allowing unlimited minting of TAF tokens that would dilute the value of TAF held by the kTAF cToken and harm depositors.
impact: kTAF depositors are exposed to inflationary dilution of the underlying TAF token. If the TAF owner mints a large amount of TAF, the DAI backing per TAF decreases, reducing the real value of kTAF deposits. Additionally, if TAF is used as collateral in the comptroller, the oracle price (fixed at $1) would not reflect the dilution, allowing over-borrowing against devalued collateral.

steps: cast call 0xf573E6740045b5387F6d36a26B102C2adF639af5 'owner()(address)' --rpc-url <RPC>
Returns 0x5a995f45CcE03670D94a0c89572dc74598d57581 (non-zero owner)
cast call 0xf573E6740045b5387F6d36a26B102C2adF639af5 'totalSupply()(uint256)' --rpc-url <RPC>
Currently 3.3e21 TAF (3300 tokens with 18 decimals)

paths.description: Exploit: owner mints TAF to drain kTAF market via redemption
steps: 1. Users deposit TAF into kTAF market, receiving kTAF cTokens
2. TAF owner calls mintAssetTokens(largeAmount) to massively inflate supply
3. TAF owner uses inflated TAF to redeem from kTAF at the current exchange rate
4. The kTAF market's TAF reserves are drained by the inflated TAF
5. Legitimate kTAF holders are left with devalued cTokens

title: Single EOA admin controls all critical contracts including cToken, comptroller proxy, and oracle
claim: The same EOA address 0xa13b3E79f2ed49BC05Af2274dC509D73a75cAFE2 serves as the admin for the kTAF cToken, the Unitroller comptroller proxy (at slot 0), and the poster/admin for the PriceOracleOTL. This creates a single point of failure where compromise of one private key grants full control over all protocol functions.
impact: If the admin EOA private key is compromised, an attacker can: (1) change the cToken's comptroller to a malicious one via _setComptroller, (2) change the Unitroller's implementation to steal all funds via _setPendingImplementation + _become, (3) manipulate oracle prices via setUnderlyingPrice/setDirectPrice to enable borrowing against worthless collateral or trigger false liquidations.

steps: cast call 0xf5140fc35c6f94d02d7466f793feb0216082d7e5 'admin()(address)' --rpc-url <RPC>
Returns 0xa13b3E79f2ed49BC05Af2274dC509D73a75cAFE2
cast storage 0x959Fb43EF08F415da0AeA39BEEf92D96f41E41b3 0x0 --rpc-url <RPC>
Returns 0xa13b3E79f2ed49BC05Af2274dC509D73a75cAFE2
cast call 0x67718a8ccc6B75FfB81D1A06Ff1cd8D97C075C67 'admin()(address)' --rpc-url <RPC>
Returns 0xa13b3E79f2ed49BC05Af2274dC509D73a75cAFE2

paths.description: Exploit: compromised admin key drains all markets
steps: 1. Attacker gains admin EOA private key
2. Attacker sets oracle prices to extreme values via setUnderlyingPrice or setDirectPrice
3. Attacker borrows all assets against minimal collateral
4. OR attacker changes comptroller implementation to drain funds directly
5. All user deposits across all 17 markets are at risk
